title: goal_scope_complete run goal_verify
purpose: A2.4 defines goal scope complete run goal verify for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified.
scope: Owns `A2.4` only; siblings under `A2` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §3 — Branch A — Pursuit & control plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#3-branch-a-pursuit-control-plane).
behavior_steps: When machine-checkable scope completion is true—task queue empty, task cards marked done, required evidence paths present—the conductor stops scheduling implement phases and pivots pursuit toward goal-level verification.
Preflight consults goal_scope_complete predicates derived from state.json and task rollups; the conductor does not invoke goal_verify on a guess or mid-stream partial completion.

On scope-complete signal, pursuit sets goal.state to verifying and runs goal_verify_command from [A1.3](A1.3-goal-verify-command-meta-test.md) instead of advancing next_action to another implement task.
Early goal_verify invocations while scope remains incomplete produce structured failures that keep goal.state in pursuing; deferred scope completion leaves the goal pursuing until predicates pass.
If scope signals conflict—queue shows empty but evidence is missing or H2 blockers remain—pursuit halts at H2 rather than entering verifying or claiming achievement without proof.

Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
goal_verify passes but H3 rejected â€” goal.state returns to pursuing with rejection notes.
Edge case `A2.4` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `A2.4`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Add `goal` block to state template and journal-keeper dual-write (v2.14).
Implement `scripts/goal-verify.py` stub aggregating task evidence paths.
Document `A2.4` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `A2.4`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: A2.4 defines goal scope complete run goal verify for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified. In practice, the system when machine-checkable scope completion is true—task queue empty, task cards marked done, required evidence paths present—the conductor stops scheduling implement phases and pivots pursuit toward goal-level verification, then preflight consults goal_scope_complete predicates derived from state.json and task rollups; the conductor does not invoke goal_verify on a guess or mid-stream partial completion.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent pilot 2026-06-28 -->

When all in-scope work for the goal is complete, pursuit shifts from step execution to goal-level proof. This capability defines the handoff: detect scope completion, then run goal_verify_command rather than another implement task.

Scope completion is machine-checkable—queue empty, task cards done, evidence present—not a conductor guess. Running goal_verify too early produces false failures; running it never leaves goals stuck in pursuing forever.

This node connects Plane A to Plane G. It is the pivot from “doing work” to “proving the goal.” Operators should expect a visible state transition to verifying before H3 is requested.
